Bios

Author Bios

One-Line Bio

~20 words · For event programs, bylines

Kat Falls is a New York Times bestselling author of adventure novels for middle grade and young adult readers, including Dark Life and Inhuman.

Short Bio

~75 words · For conference programs, press releases

Kat Falls is a New York Times bestselling author whose novels blend real science with fast-paced adventure. Her debut Dark Life — a subsea Western set on a flooded frontier — was named an Al Roker TODAY Show Book Club pick and optioned by Disney. Its sequel, Rip Tide, followed in 2011. Her YA series opener Inhuman earned a Kirkus starred review. She holds an MFA from Northwestern University, where she teaches screenwriting, and lives in Evanston, Illinois.

Long Bio

~200 words · For author pages, interviews, media kits

Kat Falls is a New York Times bestselling author of action-packed science fiction for readers ages 9 and up. Her debut novel Dark Life (Scholastic Press, 2010) — a "subsea Western" following 15-year-old Ty on the ocean-floor frontier of a flooded future Earth — was named an Al Roker TODAY Show Book Club pick in July 2010, won the 2011 Friends of American Writers Juvenile Literary Award, and was optioned by Disney with Robert Zemeckis (ImageMovers/The Gotham Group) attached to direct. The sequel, Rip Tide, arrived in 2011; both books are Junior Library Guild Selections.

Her YA duology — Inhuman (2013) and Undaunted (2019) — follows Lane McEvoy through a world split by a virus that splices human and animal DNA. Inhuman earned a Kirkus starred review and appeared on the Tome Society It List 2014–15. The series has been translated into approximately 18 languages.

A WGA member with screenwriting credits at Disney and four optioned independent screenplays, Falls holds a BS from Rensselaer Polytechnic, an MA from The New School, and an MFA in Writing for the Screen and Stage from Northwestern University, where she now teaches as faculty. She also teaches at StoryStudio Chicago and is a Ragdale Foundation resident. She lives in Evanston, Illinois with her husband, Tony Award–winning director Robert Falls, and their three children.

Key Credentials for Editorial Use

  • ✦ New York Times Bestselling Author
  • ✦ Al Roker TODAY Show Book Club Pick (July 2010)
  • ✦ 2011 Friends of American Writers Juvenile Literary Award Winner
  • ✦ Junior Library Guild Selection (Dark Life & Rip Tide)
  • ✦ Kirkus Starred Review (Inhuman)
  • ✦ Disney Film Option — Dark Life (Robert Zemeckis / ImageMovers / The Gotham Group; in development)
  • ✦ ~18 Language Translations
  • ✦ 10-State Award Nominations (Dark Life)
  • ✦ Tome Society It List 2014–15 (Inhuman)
  • ✦ MFA, Northwestern University — Now Faculty
  • ✦ WGA Member — Prior Disney Screenwriting Credits
Kat Falls

NYT bestselling author of Dark Life and Inhuman.
Evanston, IL.
